tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.natural_sourc: Natural source: realistic deposit/vein material; process it in industrial machines.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.material.head: Earth on Minecraft material: follow the next step; no chemistry memorization required.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.material.note: Tip: right-click the Field Geology Notebook for the full route guide.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.hold_shi: Hold Shift for the material card: form, composition, source, processing, and use.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.m: Mined from deposits, rocks, or veins; real material usually contains gangue and impurities.
Produced by beneficiation, roasting, reduction, smelting, or alloying.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.c: Derived from coal, coking, carbonization, or functional carbon processing.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.p: Derived from crude oil, coal tar, or hydrocarbon fraction processing.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.r: Derived from rare-earth sands, bastnasite/monazite, and hydrometallurgical separation.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.g: Produced by gas separation, cracking, combustion byproducts, or syngas routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.b: Derived from wood, pulp, natural latex, or organic polymer feedstocks.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.e: Collected from soil, water, brine, deposits, sediments, or treatment residues.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.m: Made by assembly; usually layered or composited from several material families.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.source.i: Produced by inorganic or organic chemical reaction, separation, crystallization, or byproduct routes.
Crush and mill first, then use magnetic separation, sieving, flotation, leaching, roasting, or reduction.
Focused on reduction, smelting, alloying, and byproduct separation to connect back to ingots, blooms, wire, and machines.
Uses reactors, electrolytic cells, absorption towers, and crystallizers for acid/base/salt/fertilizer routes.
Needs gas separation, absorption, synthesis loops, or acts as fuel/reducing gas.
Uses coking, distillation, cracking, combustion, or carbon-material processing to split fuels and chemical feedstocks.
Uses reaction, extraction, polymerization, and finishing to make solvents, monomers, resins, rubber, or fibers.
Produced through chlor-alkali chemistry, oxidation, saponification, surfactant blending, or water treatment.
Prepared by precipitation, impregnation, calcination, reduction, or loading active phases on a support.
Crack the ore, leach, precipitate, extract, then make oxides, salts, phosphors, or magnetic materials.
Raises purity from metallurgical silicon through chlorosilane refining, polysilicon, wafers, and trace doping.
Feeds power/electronics assembly as conductor, insulation, electrode, electrolyte, or substrate.
Mainly depends on mixing, calcining, sintering, melting, or refractory heat treatment.
Uses precipitation, neutralization, softening, stabilization, or soil amendment.
Uses chipping, pulping, bleaching, drying, or chemical modification to make pulp, fibers, and natural polymers.
General intermediate; check the next-step hint or JEI for the active route.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.meta: Makes metals, alloys, machine casings, wire, or vanilla-compatible ingot outputs.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.chem: Acts as reagent, solvent, gas, acid/base/salt, or separation medium.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.cata: Unlocks or accelerates synthesis, oxidation, hydrogenation, contact-process, and electronics routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.agri: Feeds fertilizer, soil amendment, or bone-meal-compatible agriculture loops.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.cons: Feeds glass, ceramics, cement, refractories, and construction outputs.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.ener: Feeds fuels, power, coking, carbon materials, or petrochemical feedstocks.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.mate: Feeds plastics, rubber, fibers, coatings, and modern material parts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.powe: Feeds the power grid, batteries, cables, electronics, and control systems.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.elec: Feeds circuit boards, wafers, magnets, phosphors, and high-purity material routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.trea: Used for water treatment, tailings stabilization, slag use, or environmental loops.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.spec: Preserves rare minerals, pigments, gems, redstone, or vanilla-compatible outputs.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.use.gene: Industrial-chain intermediate; machine routes decide the later use.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.simplify: Real purity still varies; this item represents a stackable pure material or standard industrial grade.
Real material is a mixture or grade range; one item represents a manageable average composition.
Real systems would use gas/fluid lines; v1 uses stackable gas cells to reduce inventory pressure.
Real structure is multilayer or multiphase; functional layers are merged into one craftable item.
For vanilla compatibility, real mineral chemistry is blended with MC redstone/gem fantasy phases.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.n: Elemental or near-elemental material; stackable pure matter in MC terms.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.m: Metal element; feeds ingots, wire, alloys, and machine parts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.a: Alloy; a simplified item for a multi-element solid solution or microstructure.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.o: Oxide; common in minerals, pigments, refractories, and metallurgy intermediates.
Oxide ore; usually crushed, milled, separated, then reduced.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Sulfide; often needs flotation, roasting, or hydrometallurgy.
Sulfide ore; commonly carries sulfur, iron, and target metals for multi-output routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Carbonate; can be calcined, acid-leached, or used in cement and soil routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Silicate or silica material; used in glass, ceramics, and rock processing.
Mixed silicates; naturally complex, simplified into stackable powders here.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.m: Mixed rock or pipe material; a mineral assemblage, not one clean formula.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.v: Hydrothermal vein material; target minerals are hosted in quartz or carbonate gangue.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.f: A compromise between real minerals and MC fantasy phases for vanilla compatibility.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.t: Tailings; low-grade residue that can be stabilized or reprocessed later.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Concentrate; enriched compared with ore, but not a pure substance.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.r: Roasted intermediate after thermal treatment of sulfides or carbonates.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Slag; smelting byproduct with silicates, oxides, and residual metals.
Salt; usually from brine, neutralization, or crystallization routes.
Mixed salt or deposit; contains multiple ions, simplified for inventory handling.
Sulfate; common in fertilizer, gypsum, water treatment, and byproduct routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.a: Acid; used for leaching, neutralization, absorption, and inorganic chemistry.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.b: Base or alkaline material; used for neutralization, precipitation, paper, fertilizer, or water treatment.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.g: Gas cell; item-based placeholder for a future fluid/pipe system.
Mixed gas; usually processed by separation, absorption, or synthesis loops.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.f: Fertilizer or nutrient salt; connects back to bone meal and agriculture loops.
Compound fertilizer mix; simplified N/P/K salts plus filler.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Cement system; multi-phase clinker plus gypsum rather than a single formula.
Clay mineral; useful for ceramics, refractories, soils, and aluminosilicates.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.g: Glass or fiberglass system dominated by a silicate network plus modifiers.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Coal or carbon-rich material; fixed carbon, volatiles, tar, and ash drive different routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.p: Petroleum or coal-tar mixture; many hydrocarbons simplified into fractions and byproducts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.m: Monomer; feeds the polymerizer to make resin or fiber materials.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.p: Polymer; one item stands in for many repeated structural units.
Polymer composite; usually resin plus filler, plasticizer, or pigment.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Solvent or organic intermediate for reactions, extraction, or polymer precursors.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.r: Reactive organic intermediate for resins, synthetic materials, and chemical routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.b: Biomass; contains cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin, or natural polymers.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.p: Pigment or filler for dyes, coatings, and building decoration outputs.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Functional carbon material for adsorption, conductivity, fuels, or batteries.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.b: Battery material simplified into electrodes, electrolyte, and cell items.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.w: Water sample/treatment material focused on hardness, ions, and residues.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Soil mixture of sand, silt, clay, humus, and salts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.r: Rubber system tied to natural/synthetic polymers, vulcanization, and fillers.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Cable composite: conductor, insulation, and redstone control layer for EOU lines.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.s: Semiconductor material centered on high-purity silicon, dopants, photoresist precursors, and wafers.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.r: Rare-earth material; hard to separate in reality and useful for magnets, phosphors, catalysts, and alloys.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Catalyst; a small amount changes reaction speed or route, usually active metal plus support.
Cleaning or detergent chemical; surfactant, soap base, builder, and filler work together.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.o: Oxidizer or bleach used for disinfection, bleaching, water treatment, or oxidation routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.h: Halide or chloride intermediate used for refining, chlorination, reduction, or high-purity routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.d: Assembled device made from metals, ceramics, conductors, insulation, and functional parts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.e: Electronics material based on fiberglass, resin, copper, and solder routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.chem.detail.c: Ceramic or refractory material that stays stable at high temperature.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.use: Right-click to open the Earth on Minecraft beginner handbook.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.core: Core idea: real sources + multi-step processing + MC item compatibility.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.use: Right-click: open the machine GUI; input goes upper-left, fuel lower-left, outputs on the right.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.fuel: Power: machines prefer EOU grid power from cables and batteries; local fuel is the off-grid fallback.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.redst: Redstone modes: always active / requires signal / requires no signal.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.route: Built-in routes: %s. JEI can show the full list.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.route.multipl: Multiple routes exist; right-click the matching machine or check JEI.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.route.compat: Use: compatible with the MC ecosystem or reserved for future integration routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.support.casin: Multiblock part: lower structural shell. Place it around the controller on the bottom layer as a rectangular base; formed structures switch to a continuous machine shell.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.support.pipe: Multiblock part: upper process piping. Place it above casing positions to form a pipe corridor or vessel top; formed structures switch to continuous flanged pipes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.support.contr: Multiblock part: place in front of a large machine controller; right-click the formed panel to open the connected machine.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.support.input: Functional multiblock part: may replace Machine Casing. Once formed, adjacent conveyors, hoppers, or containers can insert material/fuel into the connected machine.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.support.outpu: Functional multiblock part: may replace Machine Casing. Once formed, it hands products to adjacent conveyors or containers without relying on dropped-item physics.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.energy.genera: Power device: burns coal, coke, petroleum coke, or gas cells into Earth on Minecraft Unit power.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.energy.steam_: Power device: uses nuclear heat modules or turbine assemblies to feed the EOU grid from the nuclear route.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.energy.cable: Power device: connects generators, battery boxes, and machines into an EOU grid.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.energy.batter: Power device: buffers EOU so machines run smoothly; machines use grid power before local fuel.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.automation.co: Automation device: low belt surface with one visible controlled cargo slot. It behaves like an item-display transport, not dropped-item physics.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.no: Structure: compact single-block machine; right-click it directly.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.he: Multiblock: put the control panel one block in front. The machine is the front-bottom center; build a 3 wide x 2 deep x 2 tall casing box around and behind it. Input/output interfaces may replace casing.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.we: Multiblock: panel in front. Layer 0 is a 3 wide x 2 deep casing base; layer 1 uses process pipes in the same footprint, forming a wet vessel or reactor.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.he: Multiblock: panel in front. Layer 0 is a 3 wide x 3 deep casing body; layer 1 uses process pipes, forming a furnace or hot process line.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.ta: Multiblock: panel in front. Layer 0 is a 3 wide x 3 deep casing base; layers 1-2 use process pipes, forming a tower or synthesis column.
Running: consuming power/fuel and advancing a recipe. The block lights up, heats up, or brightens its liquid window.
Idle: no recipe progress. Common causes are missing input, full outputs, no power/fuel, missing structure, or redstone mode.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.empty_: Put material upper-left and fuel lower-left.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.missin: Missing power or fuel: connect battery/cable, or add fuel.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.fuel.t: Fuel is consumed only when input, output space, multiblock structure, and redstone mode all allow work.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.energy: When connected to copper cables or batteries, this machine consumes %s EOU/t and will not burn local fuel.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.fuel.e: Accepts coal, charcoal, coal dust, wood chips, coke, petroleum coke, gas cells, lava buckets, and similar fuels.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.unsupp: This material cannot be processed by this machine.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.struct: This compact machine does not need extra blocks.
Panel in front; machine is front-bottom center; 3w x 2d x 2h all casing.
Panel in front; machine is front-bottom center; casing below, pipe above, 3w x 2d.
Panel in front; machine is front-bottom center; casing below, pipe above, 3w x 3d.
Panel in front; machine is front-bottom center; layer 0 casing, layers 1-2 pipe, 3w x 3d.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.machine.interf: Large structures no longer use six face IO settings.
Replace any casing position with an input or output interface.
Output interfaces hand products to adjacent conveyors/containers; conveyors display and carry controlled cargo.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.side.input.too: External pipes or hoppers may only insert valid material or fuel through this face.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.side.output.to: External pipes or hoppers may only extract products through this face.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.side.both.tool: This face accepts material/fuel and also exposes product extraction.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.side.off.toolt: This face blocks automatic input and output.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.side.tooltip: Click a face square to cycle: input -> output -> both -> off.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.redstone.alway: Works whenever valid input and output space exist.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.redstone.requi: Works only while receiving a redstone signal.
Pauses while receiving a redstone signal.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.redstone.toolt: Modes: always active / signal required / no signal required.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.energy.generat: Attach copper cable or a battery box nearby.
Generating: +%s EOU/t, output up to %s EOU/t
Waiting for fuel, or internal buffer is currently enough.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.energy.state.r: Running: burning fuel and producing EOU. Terminals and front panels visibly light up.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.energy.state.i: Idle: not generating. It usually needs fuel, has a full buffer, or has no useful grid target.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.energy.generat: Burns fuel for %s EOU/t and exports up to %s EOU/t to battery boxes through cables.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.energy.battery: Connect cables to power nearby machines.
Machines draw from adjacent or cable-connected battery boxes; local fuel is only used when grid power is missing.
message.earth_on_minecraft.control_panel: This panel is not connected yet: place it directly in front of a machine controller, and translucent structure projections will show the missing blocks.
This part is not formed yet: machine block is the front-bottom center, panel goes in front, casing on the bottom layer, pipes above.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.title: Earth on Minecraft Field Geology Notebook
screen.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.subti: For non-technical players: follow routes; no chemistry exam required.
screen.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.foote: Mouse wheel to read; machine GUI: upper-left input, lower-left fuel/off-grid fallback, right-side outputs.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.processing.fuel: Power: prefers EOU grid; off-grid fuel accepts coal/dust/coke/petroleum coke
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.1: Not a chemistry exam: it gives day-one iron, first machine, and later industry steps.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.2: Recipe: one dirt, any plank, or any stone.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.3: First iron: mine magnetite, then smelt magnetite chunks in a vanilla furnace or blast furnace.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.4: First machine: the Jaw Crusher uses stone, cobblestone, and a furnace. No iron required.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.5: First copper: chalcopyrite chunks can also be smelted first, then craft the Ball Mill.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.notebook.6: JEI integration: notebook, machines, and materials share the same route hints.
Combustion generators burn common fuels; steam turbine generators use nuclear heat modules or turbine assemblies. Place next to a battery box or connect one with copper cable.
Beginner order: generator -> copper cable -> battery box -> processing machine; the nuclear route later feeds a steam turbine generator.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.energy.cable.0: Extends the EOU grid; machines scan adjacent cable networks for batteries and generators.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.energy.cable.1: Thin, normal, and heavy cables all carry EOU; thickness is for readable routing and machine rooms.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.energy.battery.0: Buffers power and feeds machines. Machines do not consume local fuel while grid power is available.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.energy.battery.1: If a machine stops, check battery energy, cable connection, redstone mode, and output space.
jei.earth_on_minecraft.route.footer: If there are many routes, check the matching machine category in JEI or right-click the notebook.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line0: ====== Earth on Minecraft Field Geology Notebook ======
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line1: Goal: keep MC ingots, gems, and crafting magic, but make mineral sources, rocks, deposits, and processing routes more realistic.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line2: 1. First iron: mine poor magnetite/magnetite, get magnetite chunks, then smelt them in a vanilla furnace or blast furnace.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line3: 2. First machine: Jaw Crusher = stone + cobblestone + furnace, no iron required; recover it with a stone pickaxe.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line4: 3. First copper: mine chalcopyrite; chalcopyrite chunks can also be smelted directly.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line5: 4. First Ball Mill: 1 iron ingot + 1 copper ingot + stone + furnace, then start powder processing.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line6: 5. Stable route: ore -> crusher -> chunks -> ball mill -> powder -> separation/flotation/roasting/reduction.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line7: 6. Rocks are mixtures: granite can yield quartz, feldspar, mica, monazite, and tailings.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line8: Tip: furnace shortcuts prevent early deadlocks; machines give better yield and byproducts.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line9: Open the notebook for page-by-page tutorials; JEI also lists every machine category.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line10: No chemistry knowledge required: right-click a machine, input upper-left, grid power first; lower-left fuel is the off-grid fallback.
guide.earth_on_minecraft.line11: Power route: Combustion Generator -> Copper Power Cable -> Battery Box -> processing machine; missing structures are shown in the GUI.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.beginner.firs: Early-game fallback: smelt this directly into iron first; the standard route is still crush -> mill -> separate/reduce.
Early-game fallback: magnetite dust can be smelted directly, but stable production should return to beneficiation and reduction.
Early-game fallback: smelt this directly into copper first; the standard route is still crush -> mill -> concentrate/refine.
Early-game fallback: chalcopyrite dust can be smelted directly, but larger production should move into flotation, roasting, and refining.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.beginner.jaw_: First machine: start with stone, cobblestone, and a furnace. No iron required; it is the first bridge from natural deposits into the industrial chain.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.beginner.ball: Second machine: smelt 1 iron and 1 copper first, then craft it. It turns chunks into separable powders and starts real beneficiation.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.deposit.bitum: Bituminous coal-bearing rock: higher volatiles; crushing favors coal tar, coal gas, and coal chemistry routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.deposit.anthr: Anthracite coal-bearing rock: higher fixed carbon; crushing favors coal dust, graphite, and high-heat fuel routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.fuel.coal_dus: Machine fuel: coal dust is good early power, or keep it for coal chemistry.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.fuel.coke: Efficient machine fuel: coke burns longer and represents a reducing atmosphere source.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.fuel.petroleu: High-heat machine fuel: petroleum coke comes from crude oil and refinery routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.fuel.gas_cell: Machine fuel: gas cells are portable placeholders for future fluid and pipe energy.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.steel_blo: Industrial role: heavy metal intermediate for machine casings, process piping, load-bearing parts, and vanilla iron-compatible outputs.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.printed_c: Industrial role: control and electronics core for panels, while also staying compatible with repeaters, comparators, and observers.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.simple_ba: Industrial role: early storage unit for battery boxes and rail power, while still preserving a vanilla redstone fallback.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.rubber: Industrial role: sealing and elastic material; first compatible with sticky pistons and leads, later with fluid, pressure, and insulation equipment.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.construct: Industrial role: building-material output for concrete, smooth stone, and modern industrial architecture.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.fertilize: Industrial role: nutrient output; currently connects back to bone meal first, then expands into the larger soil and fertilizer system.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.soil: Industrial role: surface and agriculture intermediate feeding fertilizer, clay, brine, and terrain-recovery branches.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.irrigatio: Industrial role: irrigation and evaporation residue that can be split back into calcite, gypsum, and salts.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.fiber: Industrial role: light-industry fiber material for paper, string, textiles, and insulation filler routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.pigment: Industrial role: pigment and filler output that first stays compatible with MC dyes, then feeds coatings and decorative materials.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.jaw_c: Inputs natural ore and deposit blocks, breaks them into coarse chunks, and releases gangue or tailings as the first industrial step.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.ball_: Inputs chunks and rocks, mills them into separable powders, and prepares feed for screening, magnetic separation, flotation, and reduction.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.sieve: Inputs uneven powders or chunks, performs size and density cleanup, and reduces the load on downstream recovery steps.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.magne: Inputs iron-bearing powders and pulls out magnetite, hematite, and mafic fractions for iron routes and tailings reduction.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.flota: Inputs sulfide and gem-bearing powders, concentrates target minerals by surface chemistry, and pushes copper, gold, lapis, and redstone into concentrate routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.ore_r: Inputs sulfides, cinnabar, and carbonate intermediates, heat-treats them, and prepares sulfur, lime, and roasted feed for reduction or leaching.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.reduc: Inputs concentrates, roasted feed, and carbon reductants, then returns them to MC-compatible metal ingots while producing slag.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.leach: Inputs veins, concentrates, or soluble intermediates, then selectively leaches target metals and salts into solution routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.elect: Inputs conductive solutions, concentrates, or salt intermediates and uses electrochemistry for metal refining plus gas and inorganic chemistry branches.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.powde: Inputs powders, concentrates, or composite feed and compresses them into stackable products as a bridge between realistic processing and MC crafting.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.chemi: Inputs acids, bases, salts, solvents, and organic/inorganic intermediates for neutralization, leaching, oxidation, condensation, and other general reactions.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.disti: Inputs brines, acids, crude oil, and coal-tar streams, then separates them by volatility into cleaner fractions and feedstocks.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.mixer: Inputs powders, master batches, and liquid additives, then blends them into glass batch, cement raw meal, fertilizer feeds, and composites.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.cryst: Inputs solutions or wet intermediates and organizes salts, caustic soda, ammonium nitrate, gypsum, and similar products into transportable solids.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.indus: Inputs materials that need calcination, sintering, or thermal dehydration and serves glass, cement, ceramics, bauxite, and refractory routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.gas_s: Inputs coal gas, air, chlorine-bearing offgas, or syngas and recovers hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, acid gases, and later reaction feedstocks by component.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.ferti: Inputs nitrogen, phosphate, and compound fertilizer feeds and organizes loose nutrients into transportable, storable granules.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.polym: Inputs ethylene, propylene, vinyl chloride, styrene, and other monomers, then links them into common plastic and resin routes.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.steam: Inputs naphtha, coal tar, diesel fractions, or gas feeds and thermally cracks them into olefins, aromatics, and syngas entry points.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.synth: Inputs hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methanol feed, and related intermediates for high-pressure loop chemistry and bulk industrial synthesis.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.machine.absor: Inputs acidic or chlorine-bearing tail gas and recovers it into hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, alkali salts, and treatment byproducts.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.intro.l:
Earth on Minecraft keeps Minecraft's crafting magic and item ecosystem, but makes natural sources and processing more realistic.
It changes ores, rocks, deposits, coal seams, kimberlite pipes, and industrial routes instead of deleting familiar vanilla outputs.

Notebook recipe: one dirt, any plank, or any stone creates the Field Geology Notebook.
Core loop: find a deposit -> process it in machines -> receive Minecraft-compatible products.
Machines work like furnaces: input upper-left, grid power first, lower-left fuel as an off-grid fallback.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.day_one:
0. Craft this notebook from one dirt, any plank, or any stone.
1. Make a stone pickaxe and a vanilla furnace. Earth on Minecraft keeps the furnace as the beginner bridge.
2. Find poor magnetite or magnetite. They are black-gray early iron sources and can be mined with stone tools.
3. Smelt magnetite chunks directly in a vanilla furnace or blast furnace before you have machines.
4. Magnetite chunk -> furnace/blast furnace -> first minecraft:iron_ingot.
5. Craft the Jaw Crusher from stone, cobblestone, and a furnace. It needs no iron and can be recovered with a stone pickaxe.
6. Find chalcopyrite and smelt its chunks for the first copper ingot.
7. One iron + one copper + stone + furnace makes the Ball Mill, then the real powder and separation route begins.
8. Machine routes give better yield and more byproducts; furnace smelting is only the early deadlock breaker.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.route_i: 3. If you have this, where does it go?

Ore/deposit blocks: start in the Jaw Crusher.
Beginner note: magnetite chunks and chalcopyrite chunks can be smelted first for your first iron and copper.
Stable production: chunks go to the Ball Mill for powders.
Magnetite, hematite, and mafic silicate powders go to the Magnetic Separator.
Chalcopyrite, pyrite, gold, lapis, and redstone materials go to the Flotation Cell.
Copper concentrate, pyrite, cinnabar, and calcite go to the Ore Roaster.
Iron concentrate, roasted copper concentrate, and gold concentrate go to the Reduction Furnace.
Salt goes to the Electrolytic Cell or Crystallizer for chlor-alkali and brine routes.
Coal dust, coal tar, and coal gas go into gas separation, distillation, and steam cracking.
Sulfur, ammonia, phosphate rock, methanol, ethylene, and potash feed the Chemical Reactor.
Feldspar, mica, and aluminosilicate powders feed sieving or kiln routes.
Cement powder and resins feed the Powder Press for MC-compatible outputs.
When unsure, hover the item. Earth on Minecraft tooltips show the next machine.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.machine:
Jaw Crusher: ore/deposit -> chunks plus tailings or associated minerals.
Beginner machine: stone + cobblestone + furnace, no iron required.
Ball Mill: chunks/rocks -> powders, usually the second step.
The first Ball Mill needs one iron ingot and one copper ingot.
Sieve: simplified size and density separation.
Magnetic Separator: pulls iron-bearing fractions out of magnetic or mafic powders.
Flotation Cell: concentrates sulfides and gem minerals.
Ore Roaster: heat-treats sulfides and carbonates.
Reduction Furnace: converts concentrates back to MC-compatible ingots and slag.
Leaching Tank and Electrolytic Cell cover hydrometallurgy and electrochemical refining.
Powder Press: turns powders or concentrates back into stackable MC-compatible items.
Chemical machines include reactor, distillation column, mixer, crystallizer, kiln, gas separator, granulator, and polymerizer.
Steam cracker, synthesis loop, and absorption tower support plastics, ammonia, urea, and offgas recovery.

Large multiblock structures:
Direction rule: the controller is the front-bottom center; place the Control Panel in front, width expands left/right, depth extends backward.
Heavy frame: 3 wide x 2 deep x 2 high casing box; input/output interfaces may replace casing.
Wet vessel/reactor: 3 wide x 2 deep x 2 high, casing below and pipes above.
Heated line: 3 wide x 3 deep x 2 high, casing below and pipes above.
Tall column: 3 wide x 3 deep x 3 high, casing below and pipes above.
Large structures do not use six face IO settings. Replace any casing position with an input or output interface, then place conveyors, hoppers, or containers next to the interface.
Output interfaces hand products to adjacent conveyors or containers. Conveyors display and move one controlled cargo item like an item-frame transport, not dropped-item physics.
Right-click any formed part to open the connected machine. Machines prefer the EOU grid; local fuel is the fallback.
JEI shows Earth on Minecraft processing categories and fuel hints.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.power_o:
Earth on Minecraft Unit, or EOU, is the first built-in power unit. It keeps the core playable without guessing another tech mod API.
Minimal grid: Combustion Generator -> Copper Power Cable -> Battery Box -> any processing machine.
Generator: burns coal, coal dust, coke, petroleum coke, gas cells, or lava into EOU.
Battery Box: buffers power. Machines draw from adjacent or cable-connected batteries before burning local fuel.
Copper Power Cable: extends the network.
Off-grid fallback: processing machines still have a lower-left fuel slot so early players do not get stuck.
No-waste rule: energy or fuel is consumed only when input, output space, structure, and redstone mode allow work.

Useful outputs:
Steel bloom crafts machine casing, process pipes, iron bars, minecarts, hoppers, and cauldrons.
Printed circuit boards craft control panels, repeaters, comparators, and observers.
Simple battery cells craft battery boxes and powered rails, or convert back to redstone.
Rubber routes craft sticky pistons, slime balls, and leads.
Cement and asphalt create modern building materials.
Fertilizer becomes bone meal; pulp, fiber, and mineral wool become paper, string, and wool.
Carbon black, titanium dioxide, and iron oxide pigments become MC dyes.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.iron_co:
Beginner shortcut:
Magnetite chunk/dust -> furnace or blast furnace -> iron ingot.
Chalcopyrite chunk/dust -> furnace or blast furnace -> copper ingot.
Use this for the first tools and machines; use the industrial route for yield and byproducts.

Iron:
Magnetite ore Fe3O4 -> crusher -> magnetite chunks + tailings.
Magnetite chunks -> Ball Mill -> magnetite dust.
Magnetite dust -> Magnetic Separator -> iron concentrate + tailings.
Iron concentrate -> Reduction Furnace -> minecraft:iron_ingot + slag.

Copper:
Chalcopyrite CuFeS2 -> crusher -> chalcopyrite chunks + pyrite dust.
Chalcopyrite chunks -> Ball Mill -> chalcopyrite dust.
Chalcopyrite dust -> Flotation Cell -> copper concentrate + pyrite dust + tailings.
Copper concentrate -> Ore Roaster -> roasted copper concentrate + sulfur.
Roasted copper concentrate -> Reduction Furnace -> copper ingot + slag.
Copper concentrate can also go through electrolysis for copper, sulfur, and slag.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.valuabl:
Gold: auriferous quartz vein -> crushing/sieving/leaching -> gold dust or concentrate -> reduction/electrolysis -> gold ingot.
Diamond: diamondiferous kimberlite -> crusher -> kimberlite chunk + diamond grit; diamond grit -> powder press -> diamond.
Lapis: lapis ore -> flotation -> lapis concentrate + calcite + pyrite; concentrate -> electrolysis -> lapis lazuli.
Emerald: beryl vein -> leaching -> beryl concentrate; concentrate -> electrolysis -> emerald + aluminosilicate dust.
Redstone keeps some Minecraft fantasy chemistry while still using mineral processing routes.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.rocks.l:
Granite, diorite, andesite, deepslate, tuff, sandstone, basalt, and blackstone are mixed rocks, not one clean formula.
Granite usually contains quartz, feldspar, mica, and small iron oxides; milling can yield quartz dust, feldspar dust, mica dust, and tailings.
Calcite and dripstone mainly represent CaCO3, which can become calcite dust and then lime dust CaO.
Basalt and blackstone give mafic silicate dust and may release magnetite or hematite.
Real separation is never perfectly pure; Minecraft lets machines organize mixtures into stackable powders so the inventory stays playable.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.chemica:
Salt -> Electrolytic Cell: sodium hydroxide, chlorine, and hydrogen.
Sulfur -> Chemical Reactor: sulfuric acid route.
Ammonia + nitric acid -> ammonium nitrate and later fertilizers.
Phosphate rock -> acidulation -> phosphoric acid and superphosphate.
Calcite, clay, silica, and gypsum feed cement and glass industries.
Coal tar, naphtha, and gas feeds go into distillation and cracking.
The first version uses item cells for gases and stackable solids for solutions.
This keeps the chemistry readable before a future fluid pipe system.
JEI is the exact recipe source; the notebook explains the route logic.
Shift tooltips show material form, composition, source, processing, and use.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.fertili:
Ammonia and nitric acid form ammonium nitrate.
Sulfuric acid and phosphate rock produce phosphoric acid and superphosphate.
Potash and potassium chloride represent potassium nutrient sources.
Urea comes from ammonia and carbon dioxide through the synthesis loop.
Fertilizer blend and granulated fertilizer connect back to bone meal for vanilla compatibility.
Later versions can add crop-specific nutrients without forcing player body systems into core.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.plastic:
Coal tar, naphtha, diesel fraction, and gas feeds enter the Steam Cracker.
Cracking gives ethylene, propylene, benzene, syngas, and other organic feedstocks.
Ethylene -> polymerizer -> polyethylene resin.
Propylene -> polymerizer -> polypropylene resin.
Vinyl chloride -> polymerizer -> PVC resin.
Styrene -> polymerizer -> polystyrene resin.
Ethylene glycol + terephthalic acid -> PET resin.
Butadiene, acrylonitrile, and styrene support ABS and synthetic rubber.
Polyol + isocyanate -> polyurethane foam.
The first version keeps recipes broad, then can split catalysts and exact ratios later.
Powder Press converts several resins into MC-compatible items so the route has early uses.
These outputs should become useful in machines, cables, insulation, seals, and later mod integrations.
The route is intentionally broad: common modern industry first, exact plant design later.
JEI remains the place for exact machine and output counts.
Tooltips explain whether a material is a monomer, polymer, solvent, or composite.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.paper_p:
Wood chips -> pulping -> cellulose pulp.
Cellulose pulp -> bleaching -> bleached pulp and cellulose fiber.
Cellulose fiber connects back to paper, string, and later textiles.
Titanium dioxide, carbon black, and iron oxide pigment feed paint and dye routes.
Paint base combines resin, pigment, and solvent.
This keeps flowers, biomass, and mineral pigments useful without forcing a full dye overhaul.
Pigments can still convert to MC dyes for building and decoration.
Future coating routes can use these as surface-treatment inputs.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.petrole:
Crude oil sample represents a broad hydrocarbon mixture, not a single substance.
Distillation separates naphtha, kerosene, diesel fraction, lubricating oil, asphalt, and petroleum coke.
Naphtha and diesel fraction feed steam cracking for olefins and aromatics.
Kerosene and diesel can later become fuels or fluids.
Lubricating oil is a future machine-maintenance route.
Asphalt connects to roads and black building materials.
Petroleum coke is a high-value fuel and carbon source.
This route is kept item-based until the fluid system is mature.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.battery:
Graphite, activated carbon, and battery carbon form the carbon side of the battery route.
Manganese oxide, nickel precursor, lithium salt, electrolyte, and electrode sheet represent simplified cell chemistry.
Simple battery cell is the early storage item.
Battery Box is the block-scale storage device for the EOU grid.
Electrolytic Cell also supports chlorine, hydrogen, oxygen, copper refining, and several inorganic routes.
Later versions can split battery chemistries, charge rates, and casing requirements.
For now, the goal is a readable power loop: generator -> cable -> battery -> machine.
Battery items keep compatibility with powered rails and redstone where sensible.
Shift tooltips mark battery materials as assembly and power-grid components.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.water_t:
Hard water sample contains calcium and magnesium ions.
Crystallizing hard water yields irrigation mineral deposits.
Irrigation deposits can be split into calcite, gypsum, and salts.
Acid/base neutralization creates neutral salt and treatment residues.
Activated carbon filter represents adsorption and polishing.
Sludge cake and stabilized tailings give wastes a route instead of making them pure trash.
This is not a pollution system yet; it is a material recovery loop first.
Future expansions can add fluids, heat, and environmental pressure as optional systems.
Natural latex is the early rubber source.
Latex -> raw rubber -> vulcanized rubber -> rubber compound and gaskets.
Sulfur and carbon black matter because real rubber uses vulcanization and fillers.
Rubber gasket should later gate pipes, pressure vessels, pumps, and chemical machines.
Current compatibility outputs include sticky pistons, slime balls, and leads.
Synthetic rubber comes from petrochemical monomers, so plant and petrochemical routes can meet.
This keeps early gameplay simple while leaving room for realistic machine sealing.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.organic:
Ethanol, acetic acid, acetone, phenol, methanol, and formaldehyde are common organic intermediates.
Phenol + formaldehyde leads toward phenolic resin.
Epoxy resin supports electronics, coatings, and composite materials.
Industrial solvent represents extraction, washing, and resin processing.
Urea-formaldehyde resin connects fertilizer chemistry and polymer chemistry.
These routes are broad on purpose; exact stoichiometry can be refined once the machine system is deeper.
Tooltips mark whether a material is a solvent, reactive intermediate, polymer, or composite.
JEI remains the exact recipe reference.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.alloys_:
Chromite dust, manganese oxide, and ferrosilicon support ferroalloy routes.
Ferrochrome and ferromanganese feed stainless steel and advanced steel variants.
Aluminum hydroxide -> alumina -> aluminum ingot -> aluminum alloy billet.
Magnesium dust and silicon help represent light-alloy additions.
Titanium route: titanium dioxide/titanium slag -> titanium tetrachloride -> titanium sponge.
Titanium sponge is the simplified output of a real chloride and reduction route.
Later versions can add vacuum melting, exact alloy grades, and stronger machine tiers.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.semicon:
Silica dust -> industrial kiln -> metallurgical silicon.
Metallurgical silicon -> chlorosilane -> high-purity silicon.
High-purity silicon -> polysilicon -> silicon wafer.
Dopant dust represents boron, phosphorus, arsenic, and similar trace dopants.
Photoresist precursor represents polymer and photoactive chemistry for lithography.
This does not turn Minecraft into a chip fab yet; it provides the material chain for future electronics.
Printed circuit boards and ceramic substrates are the early usable outputs.
The route is intentionally compact so players can test it without cleanrooms and hundreds of fluids.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.rare_ea:
Monazite sand and bastnasite dust represent rare-earth mineral sources.
They produce mixed rare earth oxides plus tailings and byproducts.
Mixed rare earth oxide -> crystallizer -> neodymium salt plus rare-earth tailings.
Neodymium salt -> synthesis loop -> NdFeB magnet plus ferrosilicon.
NdFeB magnet -> magnetic separator -> redstone + iron ingot as a compatibility output.
Catalyst routes use hematite, titanium slag, nickel precursor, and gold concentrate for iron, vanadium, nickel, and platinum-group catalysts.
Catalysts currently unlock ammonia, sulfuric acid, hydrogenation, nitric acid, and related routes.
Later they can become real prerequisites for advanced machines.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.chlor_a:
Salt dust -> Electrolytic Cell: sodium hydroxide, chlorine, and hydrogen.
Chlorine -> Absorption Tower: sodium hypochlorite and hydrochloric acid.
Sodium hypochlorite -> Mixer: bleaching powder plus slaked lime.
Hydrogen -> Chemical Reactor: hydrogen peroxide plus oxygen.
Wheat seeds -> Chemical Reactor: soap base plus glycerol, as a compact plant-oil saponification route.
Soap base -> Mixer: surfactant plus glycerol; surfactant -> Mixer: detergent powder plus soda ash.
Detergent powder -> Powder Press: white dye plus sodium sulfate, connecting daily chemistry back to MC dyes.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.electro:
Copper ingot -> Powder Press: copper wire. The first version uses the press instead of adding a wire-drawing machine.
Silica dust -> Industrial Kiln: fiberglass cloth plus glass.
Fiberglass cloth -> Mixer: copper clad laminate plus epoxy resin.
Copper wire -> Mixer: printed circuit board plus copper clad laminate.
Photoresist precursor -> Mixer: printed circuit board plus industrial solvent.
Tin ingot -> Mixer: solder alloy plus solder flux.
Mixed rare earth oxide -> Crystallizer: LED phosphor plus neodymium salt.
Ceramic insulator -> Mixer: ceramic substrate plus LED phosphor.
Printed circuit board -> Powder Press: redstone plus copper wire as the current compatibility output.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.lead_zi:
Tailings -> Sieve: sphalerite dust, galena dust, and silica dust. Tailings can still carry associated metals.
Sphalerite dust -> Flotation Cell: zinc oxide plus sulfur.
Zinc oxide -> Reduction Furnace: zinc ingot plus slag.
Galena dust -> Flotation Cell: lead ingot plus sulfur.
Sand -> Sieve can also yield cassiterite dust; cassiterite -> Reduction Furnace -> tin ingot plus slag.
Tin ingot -> Mixer: solder alloy plus solder flux.
Lead ingot -> Powder Press: solder alloy plus stabilized tailings, representing safer encapsulation.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.ceramic:
Clay -> Ball Mill: clay dust, kaolin dust, and aluminosilicate dust.
Clay dust -> Sieve: kaolin dust and aluminosilicate dust.
Kaolin dust -> Mixer: ceramic body plus feldspar dust.
Kaolin dust -> Industrial Kiln: refractory clay plus alumina.
Refractory clay -> Industrial Kiln: firebrick plus slag.
Ceramic body -> Industrial Kiln: porcelain blank; porcelain blank -> Powder Press: ceramic insulator.
Mafic silicate dust -> Industrial Kiln: mineral wool plus slag.
Firebrick, ceramic insulator, and ceramic substrate later become upgrades for furnaces, reactors, and electronics.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.nuclear:
Nuclear power is a real modern Earth on Minecraft core route, not a sci-fi addon. The first version uses safe stackable items, not radiation punishment or accident simulation.
Entry: monazite sand or rare-earth tailings -> sieving/leaching -> uraninite dust. Uranium appears as an associated mineral, not from nowhere.
Uraninite dust -> Leaching Tank -> yellowcake uranium oxide plus stabilized tailings.
Yellowcake -> Chemical Reactor -> uranium hexafluoride cell; UF6 -> Gas Separator -> low-enriched uranium plus depleted uranium.
Low-enriched uranium -> Chemical Reactor -> uranium dioxide powder; uranium dioxide powder -> Powder Press -> nuclear fuel pellet.
Titanium/zirconium alloy route -> Industrial Kiln -> zirconium alloy cladding tube. Borate minerals -> Reduction Furnace -> boron carbide pellet.
Fuel pellet -> Mixer -> nuclear fuel rod; fuel rod -> Mixer -> nuclear fuel assembly; boron carbide pellet -> control rod assembly.
Nuclear fuel assembly -> Synthesis Loop -> nuclear heat module plus spent fuel assembly. Put the heat module into a steam turbine generator to power the EOU grid made of copper cables, batteries, and machines.
Spent fuel assembly -> Powder Press -> dry storage cask plus depleted uranium. This represents spent-fuel pool and dry-cask concepts while staying safe for beginners.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.grid_au:
Automation is core too. It should not require players to write code; it turns seeing, deciding, acting, and feedback into understandable parts.
Grid basics: copper wire -> Powder Press -> copper busbar; busbar -> Mixer -> transformer core; transformer core -> Mixer -> grid switchgear.
Generation island: copper wire and magnet route -> generator stator; steel bloom -> Industrial Kiln -> steam turbine assembly.
Steam turbine generator: copper busbars + turbine assembly + generator stator + switchgear -> steam turbine generator. Insert a nuclear heat module to export high-output EOU to the grid.
Control entry: printed circuit board -> Mixer -> industrial sensor + PLC controller.
Motion entry: NdFeB magnet -> Mixer -> servo motor; servo motor -> Mixer -> actuator module + robotic arm.
Vision inspection: silicon wafer -> Crystallizer -> machine vision camera; camera -> Mixer -> quality inspection module.
Redstone bridge: PLC controller -> Mixer -> automation bus + redstone I/O gateway, so vanilla redstone can understand industrial machines.
Logistics entry: robotic arm -> Powder Press -> conveyor drive; conveyor drive + rubber + steel + copper wire -> industrial conveyor. Conveyors store and display one controlled cargo item, moving it like an item-frame transport; sorters, robotic arms, and interface upgrades come later.
Machines already have input/output slots, process time, redstone modes, GUI, JEI integration, grid power, steam turbine generation, conveyors, and automation materials so the realistic route can be tested.
Future layers can add multi-input ratios, energy cost, catalysts, fluids, pollution, heat, sorters, interface upgrades, transformer blocks, and nuclear multiblocks.
Common tech or magic mod integrations should not live in the vanilla-core handbook.
The sci-fi addon should grow upward from high-purity silicon, rare-earth magnets, PCB, nuclear heat, grid parts, and automation buses instead of changing core early-game difficulty.
Compatibility principle: natural sources and processing become realistic, while final outputs still use familiar vanilla items and tags.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.boundar:
Core covers common real-world Earth content: geology, deposits, rocks, soil, hydrology, modern industry, nuclear power, electricity, automation, logistics, infrastructure, handbook, and JEI routes.
Earth Human is a separate integration mod: player attributes, body temperature, nutrition, disease, food preservation, and medicine do not belong in core.
Cultivation addon: qi, spiritual roots, pills, artifacts, realms, and progression.
Magic addon: mana, elements, rituals, runes, alchemy, and magical ecology.
Sci-fi addon: space, autonomous AI, nanomanufacturing, antimatter, warp, and beyond-real energy after core stabilizes.
This split keeps core friendly to all players and modpacks while other routes can optionally change pressure or worldview.
Firearms and cannon weapon chains are excluded; materials, energy, control, transport, and safety infrastructure are allowed.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.soil_wa:
Vanilla sand remains minecraft:sand. New materials use precise names such as sandy loam, alluvial loam, and saline soil sample.
Grass block, dirt, coarse dirt, rooted dirt, podzol, and mud can be milled into humus, clay, soil mineral mix, or alluvial loam.
Sand and red sand can be sieved; red sand leans more toward hematite.
Gravel represents riverbed sediment and can yield silica, calcite, mafic silicates, hematite, and tailings.
Hard water crystallizes into irrigation mineral deposits, which break down into calcite, gypsum, and salt.
Saline soil can be leached into brine crystals, clay, and tailings.
These are material-source routes only; player body systems and food preservation belong to future addons.
handbook.earth_on_minecraft.page.vanilla:
Leaf litter -> Ball Mill: humus sample plus cellulose fiber.
Dead bush -> Ball Mill: wood chips, potash, and humus.
Bush, short grass, fern, and dry grass -> Ball Mill: humus, cellulose, or potash.
Wildflowers -> Chemical Reactor: dyes plus cellulose and humus.
Cactus flower -> Chemical Reactor: pink dye plus natural latex as a simplified waxy plant route.
Kelp and dried kelp -> Chemical Reactor: soda ash, potash, and cellulose fiber.
Principle: common vanilla ecology should feed realistic material chains instead of becoming useless inventory clutter.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.nuclear: Use: nuclear fuel-cycle and nuclear-heat route material. Safely sealed by default; no hardcore radiation punishment.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.automatio: Use: automation control, sensing, actuation, inspection, or redstone bridging. Later connects to conveyors and sorters.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.use.grid: Use: grid, generation island, transformer/switchgear, or steam-turbine route. Later upgrades into functional blocks.
tooltip.earth_on_minecraft.multiblock.le: Direction guide: the side you face in the machine GUI is the front. Put the control panel in front; width expands left/right and depth extends behind the machine.
